beats.narration: Disbonds, voids, core separations — bondline defects lie flat, parallel to the surface, at known depths. Straight-beam sound hits them square.

payload.title: READ THE BONDLINE
payload.sub: Put sound in — read what the bond sends back.

beats.narration: Three ways to ask: pulse-echo reads depth. Through-transmission maps the whole stack, no depth. Resonance probes feel the free skin move the null.

payload.heading: Three ways to interrogate a bond

beats.narration: Pulse-echo over a good bond: multiples die fast — energy drains across the adhesive. Over a disbond, the free skin rings at skin-thickness spacing.
Reading the screen: a near-side disbond rings at skin spacing. A far-side break grows an echo at the second bondline. On honeycomb, ringing means bad.
So carry the rule: good bond drains the ring, disbond rings on. Sound reports geometry, never strength — kissing bonds stay invisible.
